NVIDIA DeepStream Crowd Detection & ROI Enforcement System |  Jetson Orin Deployment 
 NVIDIA DEEPSTREAM 
Crowd Detection & ROI-Based Access Restriction System 
Deployed on NVIDIA Jetson Orin  |  Technical Architecture & Implementation Proposal 
Prepared for: Manager Review  |  April 2026  |  Version 1.0 
 
Executive Summary 
 
This document presents the technical architecture and implementation plan for an intelligent Crowd 
Detection and Region of Interest (ROI) Access Restriction System, built on NVIDIA's DeepStream 
SDK and deployed on the NVIDIA Jetson Orin edge computing platform.

ation plan for an intelligent Crowd 
Detection and Region of Interest (ROI) Access Restriction System, built on NVIDIA's DeepStream 
SDK and deployed on the NVIDIA Jetson Orin edge computing platform. The system ingests live camera feeds, runs real-time crowd density analysis using NVIDIA's pre-
trained people-detection and crowd-counting models, dynamically defines and enforces ROI zones, 
and triggers automated alerts when individuals attempt to enter restricted areas. The entire inference 
pipeline runs at the edge — on the Jetson Orin — ensuring low latency, offline capability, and data 
privacy. Primary use cases include: crowd detection and real-time monitoring in political campaigns and 
rallies for safety, turnout analysis, and resource planning.

The pipeline is composed of five integrated layers running on Jetson Orin, orchestrated by the

TAO Toolkit models

coordinate boundary logic

send webhook notifications

Logging Layer

events, export to dashboard

NVIDIA DeepStream Crowd Detection & ROI Enforcement System |  Jetson Orin Deployment 
 
Hardware — NVIDIA Jetson Orin 
 
The NVIDIA Jetson Orin is the recommended edge deployment platform for this system. Its 
dedicated GPU, deep learning accelerators (DLA), and hardware video decode engines make it 
ideal for running multiple concurrent camera streams with real-time inference.

✅ Recommendation 
For a typical deployment of 4–12 cameras with real-time crowd detection + ROI enforcement, the 
Jetson AGX Orin 32GB is recommended. It provides 200 TOPS of AI performance, supports hardware-accelerated decoding of 16 concurrent 
1080p streams, and runs the full DeepStream pipeline without frame-drop at 30 FPS per stream. For cost-sensitive single-site deployments (1–4 cameras), the Orin NX 16GB is a viable lower-cost

NVIDIA DeepStream Pipeline Architecture 
 
NVIDIA DeepStream SDK provides a GStreamer-based pipeline for building high-performance video 
analytics applications. Each stage of the pipeline is implemented as a GStreamer element (plugin), 
hardware-accelerated via CUDA and the Jetson Orin's dedicated video processing engines. Full GStreamer Pipeline Flow 
 
📡 DeepStream GStreamer Element Chain 
1.

ented as a GStreamer element (plugin), 
hardware-accelerated via CUDA and the Jetson Orin's dedicated video processing engines. Full GStreamer Pipeline Flow 
 
📡 DeepStream GStreamer Element Chain 
1. [source_bin]  →  URI source (RTSP/file/USB) per camera via uridecodebin 
2. [nvstreammux] →  Batch multiple streams into single buffer (NvBufSurface) 
3. [nvinfer]     →  Primary inference: PeopleNet detector (TensorRT engine, FP16/INT8) 
4. [nvtracker]   →  NvDCF / DeepSORT multi-object tracker — assigns unique Person IDs 
5. [nvinfer #2]  →  Secondary inference: Crowd density / attribute classification (optional) 
6. [nvdsanalytics]→ ROI polygon definition, line crossing detection, direction detection 
7. [roi_plugin]  →  Custom GStreamer plugin: checks person bbox centroid vs ROI polygons 
8. [nvosd]       →  On-screen display: bounding boxes, ROI overlays, alert banners 
9.

n, direction detection 
7. [roi_plugin]  →  Custom GStreamer plugin: checks person bbox centroid vs ROI polygons 
8. [nvosd]       →  On-screen display: bounding boxes, ROI overlays, alert banners 
9. [alert_plugin]→  Trigger MQTT/HTTP alert, GPIO relay, event log on violation 
10.[nvvideoconvert + nvv4l2h265enc] → Re-encode annotated stream for RTSP output 
11.[rtsp_sink]   →  Output annotated stream to RTSP server for dashboard / NVR viewing 
 
Detailed Pipeline Stages 
 
STEP 
1 
Multi-Camera Video Ingestion 
Multiple IP camera streams (RTSP), USB webcams, or recorded video files are ingested 
simultaneously. The NvStreamMux element batches all streams into a single 
NvBufSurface for efficient GPU batch inference.

IP camera streams (RTSP), USB webcams, or recorded video files are ingested 
simultaneously. The NvStreamMux element batches all streams into a single 
NvBufSurface for efficient GPU batch inference. • Supports up to 16 simultaneous 1080p streams on Jetson AGX Orin 
• NVDEC hardware decoder handles H.264 / H.265 decode with zero CPU load 
• Configurable input resolution, FPS, and batch size per stream 
• Supports RTSP, V4L2 (USB/CSI), file URI, and GigE Vision cameras 
 
STEP People Detection — NVIDIA PeopleNet

NVIDIA DeepStream Crowd Detection & ROI Enforcement System |  Jetson Orin Deployment 
2 
PeopleNet is an NVIDIA TAO pre-trained model optimised for detecting people in 
crowded scenes. It runs as a TensorRT-optimised engine on the Jetson Orin GPU, 
delivering real-time bounding box detections for every person in each frame.

ed model optimised for detecting people in 
crowded scenes. It runs as a TensorRT-optimised engine on the Jetson Orin GPU, 
delivering real-time bounding box detections for every person in each frame. • NVIDIA PeopleNet v2.6 (NVIDIA NGC catalogue, free to download): Model 
• ResNet-34 backbone with SSD detection head: Architecture 
• Person, Bag, Face (configurable): Classes detected 
• 960×544 (default), configurable per deployment: Input resolution 
• FP16 or INT8 (TensorRT-optimised on Jetson Orin): Precision 
• ~30 FPS per stream at 1080p on Jetson AGX Orin: Performance 
• Configurable (default: 0.6 for person class): Confidence threshold 
 
📡 Model Source 
PeopleNet is available for free on NVIDIA NGC: 
https://catalog.ngc.nvidia.com/orgs/nvidia/teams/tao/models/peoplenet 
Download the .etlt model, convert to TensorRT engine using tao-converter on the Jetson Orin. Pre-built DeepStream config files are included in the NGC download package.

Download the .etlt model, convert to TensorRT engine using tao-converter on the Jetson Orin. Pre-built DeepStream config files are included in the NGC download package. STEP 
3 
Multi-Object Tracking 
Each detected person is assigned a unique tracking ID that persists across frames. This 
enables the system to monitor individual movement paths, determine entry into ROI 
zones, and maintain per-person violation history.

is assigned a unique tracking ID that persists across frames. This 
enables the system to monitor individual movement paths, determine entry into ROI 
zones, and maintain per-person violation history. • NvDCF (NVIDIA Discriminative Correlation Filter) — GPU-accelerated, low latency: 
Primary tracker 
• DeepSORT with Re-ID embeddings for higher accuracy in dense crowds: Alternative 
• Bounding box (x, y, w, h), centroid, unique track ID, class label, confidence: Output per 
person 
• Optional OSNet / NVIDIA Re-identification model for handling occlusions: Re-ID model 
• Configurable max age (frames before track is dropped), min hits to confirm: Track 
lifecycle 
 
STEP 
4 
Crowd Density Analysis 
Beyond person detection, the system uses a crowd density estimation model to compute 
crowd density heatmaps and head counts per ROI zone. This enables proactive density-
based alerts before physical violations occur.

ection, the system uses a crowd density estimation model to compute 
crowd density heatmaps and head counts per ROI zone. This enables proactive density-
based alerts before physical violations occur. • NVIDIA CrowdCount (DmCount / CSRNet architecture, available on NGC): Model 
• Crowd density heatmap (per pixel density) + aggregated count per defined zone: 
Output 
• Configurable per ROI: e.g., alert if > 30 people/m² or > 50 people in zone: Thresholds 
• Density map updated every N frames (configurable for performance tuning): Update 
rate 
 
📡 Density Levels & Actions

NVIDIA DeepStream Crowd Detection & ROI Enforcement System |  Jetson Orin Deployment 
GREEN  (Low density)   — < 1 person/m²   — No action required 
YELLOW (Medium density)— 1–3 persons/m²  — Log event, display density overlay 
ORANGE (High density)  — 3–6 persons/m²  — Send advisory alert to operator 
RED    (Critical)      — > 6 persons/m²  — Trigger alarm, close entry, notify emergency team 
 
STEP 
5 
ROI Zone Definition & Management 
Regions of Interest (ROIs) are polygonal zones drawn onto the camera's coordinate 
space. Each ROI is classified as either RESTRICTED (no entry) or MONITORED 
(density tracking only). The ROI engine checks every tracked person's position against 
all active ROIs in real time.

space. Each ROI is classified as either RESTRICTED (no entry) or MONITORED 
(density tracking only). The ROI engine checks every tracked person's position against 
all active ROIs in real time. ROI Types

machinery area, VIP lounge,

alert

restricted zone

Warning alert + operator 
notification

ZONE

occupancy cap

exceeds threshold

gate control

One-way corridor or flow 
control area

warning overlay

presence is flagged

enforcement 
 
ROI Configuration Format 
 
ROI zones are defined in a DeepStream analytics configuration file (config_nvdsanalytics.txt).

in zone during 
restricted time window 
Alert with time-based 
enforcement 
 
ROI Configuration Format 
 
ROI zones are defined in a DeepStream analytics configuration file (config_nvdsanalytics.txt). Each 
zone is specified as a list of polygon vertices in pixel coordinates: 
 
📡 Sample ROI Config (config_nvdsanalytics.txt) 
[roi-filtering-stream-0] 
enable = 1 
roi-RF-0 = 120;200;350;200;350;480;120;480   # Restricted Zone 1 (rectangle) 
roi-RF-1 = 600;150;900;150;950;400;550;400   # Restricted Zone 2 (polygon) 
 
[overcrowding-stream-0] 
enable = 1

NVIDIA DeepStream Crowd Detection & ROI Enforcement System |  Jetson Orin Deployment 
roi-OC-0 = 0;0;1920;0;1920;1080;0;1080       # Full frame crowd monitor 
overcrowding-threshold = 50                   # Alert if > 50 persons 
 
[line-crossing-stream-0] 
enable = 1 
extended = 0 
line-crossing-Entry = 960;0;960;1080           # Vertical mid-line crossing 
 
ROIs can also be managed dynamically at runtime via a REST API endpoint served on the Jetson 
Orin, allowing operators to draw, move, or delete zones from a web dashboard without restarting the 
pipeline.

can also be managed dynamically at runtime via a REST API endpoint served on the Jetson 
Orin, allowing operators to draw, move, or delete zones from a web dashboard without restarting the 
pipeline. STEP 
6 
Violation Detection & Alert Engine 
When a tracked person's bounding box centroid enters a RESTRICTED or BUFFER ROI, 
the violation detection engine logs the event and dispatches alerts through multiple 
configured channels simultaneously.

NVIDIA DeepStream Crowd Detection & ROI Enforcement System |  Jetson Orin Deployment 
Model Deployment Workflow 
 
• Step 1 — Download PeopleNet .etlt model from NVIDIA NGC catalog 
• Step 2 — Convert .etlt to TensorRT engine on Jetson Orin using tao-converter: 
◦ tao-converter -k <key> -d 3,544,960 -p input_1,1x3x544x960,4x3x544x960,8x3x544x960 -t 
fp16 -e /models/peoplenet_fp16.engine peoplenet_v2.6.etlt 
• Step 3 — Configure DeepStream primary inference config 
(config_infer_primary_peoplenet.txt) 
• Step 4 — Configure NvDCF tracker with appropriate parameters for crowd density 
• Step 5 — Define ROI polygons in config_nvdsanalytics.txt for each camera stream 
• Step 6 — Configure alert sinks: MQTT broker address, webhook URLs, GPIO pins 
• Step 7 — Launch DeepStream application:  deepstream-app -c 
deepstream_crowd_config.txt 
• Step 8 — Verify annotated RTSP output stream on monitoring dashboard 
 
ROI Management Interface 
 
A lightweight web-based ROI Management Dashboard is deployed alongside the DeepStream 
pipeline on the Jetson Orin.

annotated RTSP output stream on monitoring dashboard 
 
ROI Management Interface 
 
A lightweight web-based ROI Management Dashboard is deployed alongside the DeepStream 
pipeline on the Jetson Orin. Operators can use this interface to draw, edit, and delete ROI zones 
directly on a live camera snapshot without editing configuration files.

Operators can use this interface to draw, edit, and delete ROI zones 
directly on a live camera snapshot without editing configuration files. Dashboard Features 
 
• Live Camera Snapshot Viewer — Display current frame from each camera for ROI 
placement 
• ROI Drawing Tool — Click-to-draw polygonal zones directly on the camera image 
• ROI Type Assignment — Set zone type: Restricted / Buffer / Crowd Limit / Direction / Dwell 
• Per-Zone Settings — Configure crowd threshold, dwell time limit, active schedule, and alert 
severity 
• Real-Time Violation Feed — Live log of detected violations with thumbnails 
• Crowd Density Heatmap View — Overlay density map on camera stream in real time 
• Person Count per Zone — Live occupancy count displayed per zone 
• Incident Replay — Access stored violation snapshots and 30-second event video clips 
• REST API — All dashboard actions also exposed via REST API for integration with 
VMS/PSIM 
 
📡 Dashboard Tech Stack 
Frontend: React.js + Fabric.js (canvas-based polygon drawing) + Leaflet.js (zone visualisation) 
Backend: FastAPI (Python) running on Jetson Orin, serving REST endpoints for ROI CRUD 
operations 
Communication: WebSocket for live violation event feed; REST for ROI config updates 
Video: RTSP-to-HLS transcoder (FFmpeg) for in-browser live camera preview 
Persistence: SQLite for ROI zone definitions; InfluxDB for time-series crowd metrics

Model Fine-Tuning with NVIDIA TAO Toolkit 
 
While PeopleNet performs well out-of-the-box, fine-tuning on site-specific data (e.g., top-down 
camera angles, low-light conditions, specific clothing or uniforms) significantly improves detection 
accuracy. NVIDIA TAO Toolkit enables fine-tuning without requiring deep ML expertise.

., top-down 
camera angles, low-light conditions, specific clothing or uniforms) significantly improves detection 
accuracy. NVIDIA TAO Toolkit enables fine-tuning without requiring deep ML expertise. • Collect 500–2,000 labelled images from the deployment site cameras 
• Annotate using NVIDIA CVAT (open-source) or Roboflow (SaaS, free tier available) 
• Run TAO fine-tuning on a cloud GPU instance (AWS/GCP/Azure) or locally on the Jetson 
• Export fine-tuned model as .etlt and re-convert to TensorRT engine for deployment 
• Expected accuracy improvement: +5–15% mAP in site-specific conditions vs base model 
 
📡 TAO Fine-Tuning Resources 
TAO Toolkit Documentation: https://docs.nvidia.com/tao/tao-toolkit/

NVIDIA DeepStream Crowd Detection & ROI Enforcement System |  Jetson Orin Deployment 
PeopleNet Fine-Tuning Guide: Available on NVIDIA Developer Zone 
Dataset annotation tool: NVIDIA CVAT — https://cvat.ai (open-source, self-hostable) 
Recommended training GPU: NVIDIA A10G (AWS g5 instance) — ~$1.00/hr for ~2–4hr training run 
Estimated fine-tuning cost: $2–$8 per training run on cloud GPU

site images; lower confidence threshold for night mode. Implementation Roadmap 
 
Phase 1 — Proof of Concept (Weeks 1–3) 
• Procure Jetson AGX Orin 32GB dev kit and 2 test IP cameras 
• Flash JetPack 6.x, install DeepStream SDK and dependencies 
• Download and convert PeopleNet .etlt to TensorRT engine 
• Run DeepStream people-detection reference app on test camera feed 
• Define 2–3 test ROI zones using nvdsanalytics config 
• Verify violation detection output on single stream at 30 FPS 
 
Phase 2 — Core Pipeline Integration (Weeks 4–7) 
• Integrate multi-camera support (4–8 streams via NvStreamMux) 
• Build custom ROI violation GStreamer plugin in Python 
• Implement MQTT alert publishing and GPIO relay trigger 
• Deploy FastAPI ROI management REST API on Jetson 
• Integrate CrowdCount model for density heatmap overlay 
• Build basic React.js dashboard for ROI drawing and live event feed 
 
Phase 3 — Production Hardening (Weeks 8–12) 
• Fine-tune PeopleNet on site-specific data using TAO Toolkit 
• Integrate InfluxDB + Grafana for crowd analytics dashboard 
• Implement alert escalation logic (email/SMS on sustained violations) 
• Add incident replay and snapshot export to dashboard 
• Deploy Docker container for reproducible rollouts across sites 
• Conduct load testing (16 streams, sustained 8-hour run) and thermal validation 
• Document deployment runbook and train security operations team 
 
Conclusion & Recommended Next Steps 
 
The proposed NVIDIA DeepStream Crowd Detection & ROI Enforcement System provides a fully 
edge-deployed, real-time solution for intelligent crowd management and access restriction.

ended Next Steps 
 
The proposed NVIDIA DeepStream Crowd Detection & ROI Enforcement System provides a fully 
edge-deployed, real-time solution for intelligent crowd management and access restriction. Leveraging NVIDIA's pre-trained PeopleNet and CrowdCount models on the Jetson Orin hardware 
platform ensures production-grade inference performance with minimal recurring cost — all primary 
software components are open-source and free. The system's ROI-based enforcement architecture is flexible enough to cover a wide range of 
scenarios — from industrial no-entry zones to stadium crowd management — and the interactive 
ROI dashboard eliminates the need for technical expertise to manage zones post-deployment.

NVIDIA DeepStream Crowd Detection & ROI Enforcement System |  Jetson Orin Deployment

• Approve hardware procurement: Jetson AGX Orin 32GB dev kit (~$999) + 2 test cameras

• Identify target deployment site and survey camera mounting positions 
• Define initial set of ROI zones in coordination with security operations team 
• Schedule Phase 1 kick-off for a 3-week proof-of-concept sprint

Retrieval augmented generation (RAG) is a design pattern that combines a retrieval subsystem with a large language model. The retriever supplies relevant source passages to the model, which then conditions answers on factual context from the source documents. RAG pipelines are widely used in search, question answering, customer support, and knowledge management systems.

ChromaDB is a vector database built for embeddings. It supports fast similarity search over dense vectors and can persist collections to disk using DuckDB and Parquet. It is ideal for production semantic search systems because it can store metadata alongside embeddings and scale across many documents.

Chunking strategies decide how text is broken into retrievable pieces. Fixed chunking uses a consistent window size with overlap. Recursive chunking splits by paragraphs and sentences to preserve semantics. Semantic chunking groups text by natural sentence boundaries so that each chunk remains coherent.

Maximum Marginal Relevance (MMR) improves retrieval by balancing relevance with diversity. It selects items that are both similar to the query and dissimilar from already chosen candidates. This helps the search engine avoid returning many overlapping snippets and surfaces broader coverage of the corpus.

A robust embedding pipeline uses batching, caching, and stable identifiers. Batching reduces API request overhead. Caching prevents redundant embedding calls on unchanged data. Storing chunk ids, text, and metadata together makes retrieval fast and reproducible.
